seo.title: Privacy-First Architecture implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Privacy-First Architecture projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical framework for validating that an application adheres to privacy-first principles before deployment, focusing on data minimization, infrastructure sovereignty, and user agency.

items.title: PII Log Sanitization
items.description: Verify that application loggers (e.g., Winston, Pino) use middleware to redact emails, physical addresses, and full names before writing to stdout or disk.

items.title: IP Address Masking
items.description: Configure reverse proxies (Nginx/Traefik) or application code to mask the last octet of IPv4 addresses and the last 80 bits of IPv6 addresses.

items.title: Database Schema Audit
items.description: Review every column in the production schema; remove any fields that are not strictly required for the application's core functionality.

items.title: Temporary Data TTLs
items.description: Set Time-To-Live (TTL) indexes on session stores and temporary caches to ensure data is automatically purged after inactivity.

items.title: Client-Side Storage Cleanup
items.description: Verify that sensitive data is cleared from localStorage or sessionStorage upon user logout or session expiration.

items.title: EU-Only Compute Region
items.description: Confirm that all cloud resources (Compute, DB, Object Storage) are provisioned exclusively in EU regions (e.g., eu-central-1) to avoid Schrems II complications.

items.title: Sub-processor Audit
items.description: Trace every outgoing network request from the production environment to ensure no data is sent to US-based services without a valid DPA.

items.title: Self-Hosted Asset Verification
items.description: Ensure all fonts, icons, and JavaScript libraries are hosted on your own infrastructure rather than via third-party CDNs like Google Fonts or cdnjs.

items.title: Database Encryption at Rest
items.description: Verify that the underlying storage for Postgres/MySQL/NoSQL instances is encrypted using AES-256 with managed or customer-provided keys.

items.title: Encrypted Backups
items.description: Test backup restoration from an encrypted snapshot to ensure that backup files are not stored in cleartext on secondary storage.

items.title: Cookie-less Analytics Setup
items.description: Verify that Plausible, Umami, or Fathom is configured to run without generating or storing persistent browser cookies.

items.title: Do-Not-Track (DNT) Respect
items.description: Validate that the analytics script or backend logic checks for the 'DNT' header and suppresses tracking if present.

items.title: Event Property Scrubbing
items.description: Scan all custom event metadata in PostHog or Matomo to ensure no dynamic user-input strings (like search queries) contain PII.

items.title: Session Recording Masking
items.description: If using session replay, verify that all input fields and sensitive text elements are masked via CSS classes before being uploaded.

items.title: Fingerprinting Prevention
items.description: Ensure that the tracking implementation does not use Canvas fingerprinting or TLS ja3 hashes to identify users across sessions.

items.title: LLM Training Opt-Out
items.description: Verify that API requests to LLM providers (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic) include the specific flag or account setting to opt-out of data training.

items.title: Local Embedding Generation
items.description: Configure the application to generate vector embeddings locally (e.g., using Transformers.js or Ollama) instead of sending raw text to cloud APIs.

items.title: PII Pre-filtering for APIs
items.description: Implement a regex-based or NLP filter to strip names and emails from user prompts before they are sent to external AI services.

items.title: Webhook Payload Minimization
items.description: Review outgoing webhooks (e.g., to Stripe or Slack) and ensure they only send the minimum required IDs rather than full user objects.

items.title: Local LLM Fallback
items.description: Implement a local model fallback for processing highly sensitive data categories that cannot leave the VPC.

items.title: Automated Data Export (SAR)
items.description: Verify that users can trigger a self-service download of all their personal data in a machine-readable format (JSON/CSV).

items.title: Hard Deletion Cascade
items.description: Confirm that a 'Delete Account' action triggers a hard delete across all primary databases, related tables, and object storage buckets.

items.title: Backup Purge Cycle
items.description: Verify that the backup retention policy is set to 30 days or less to ensure that deleted user data is eventually purged from all archives.

items.title: Consent Withdrawal Verification
items.description: Test that revoking consent for marketing or analytics immediately halts all associated data collection and processing activities.

items.title: Consent Audit Logging
items.description: Ensure that every change in user consent is logged with a timestamp and the version of the privacy policy accepted.

items.title: Privacy-First Email Provider
items.description: Verify that transactional emails are sent via privacy-respecting providers (e.g., Listmonk, Buttondown) rather than marketing-heavy platforms.

items.title: Tracking Pixel Removal
items.description: Disable open-tracking and click-tracking pixels in transactional emails to prevent leaking user IP and device data to the ESP.

items.title: Email Link Proxying
items.description: Ensure that links in emails do not include identifying tokens in the URL parameters that could be leaked to third-party sites via Referer headers.

items.title: Double Opt-In Workflow
items.description: Validate that the newsletter or notification subscription process requires a confirmed double opt-in to prevent malicious signups.

items.title: List Segmentation Privacy
items.description: Verify that email lists are segmented without exporting entire user databases to the email service provider.
